t_en: The dial tone cuts off, yet then the sound rings out again.

t_ch: 你好，我是小小玛丽，我现在 在 你 的 身  后……
t_en: Hi, this is Mini Mary, and I'm... right... behind... you...

t_ch: 不要靠近我啊啊啊——
t_en: Stay away from me, aaaaaaah——

t_ch: 用户彻底害怕了！不过说到底，小小玛丽只是应答机上的一段智能程序，并不能真的做什么。
t_en: The user is completely freaked out! But in the end, I'm just an AI program on the answering machine, so I cannot actually do anything.

t_ch: 那么，请继续支持玛丽速递——
t_en: So, please continue to support Mary Express!

t_ch: 对不起，我不应该丢下你！
t_en: I'm sorry! I shouldn't have left you behind!

t_ch: 是的，小小玛丽其实想要听到的，只是这句话而已。现在，我已经如愿了…再见了，主人，祝你幸福。
t_en: Yes, this is all I want to hear. Now, my wish has come true... Farewell, master, and may you find happiness.

t_ch: 玛丽速递，马上就到你身后～
t_en: Mary Express, right behind you already~

t_ch: 看来在二相乐园，人工智能想要取代幻造种的工作，还需要走很长一段路……
t_en: Looks like AI in Planarcadia still has a long way to go before it can replace imagenae's work...

t_ch: 电话亭里的电话铃声响个不停，要去接听一下吗？
t_en: The phone in the phone booth keeps ringing. Should you answer it?

t_ch: 接一下，说不定有事找我呢…
t_en: Take it. Perhaps someone's looking for me...

t_ch: 不是我喜欢的电话，我不接。
t_en: I don't like this call, so I won't answer it.

t_ch: 就让电话铃继续响下去吧，城市的喧嚣声里，要是少了它可不行。
t_en: Let the phone keep ringing. The city's symphony just wouldn't be complete without it.

t_ch: 喂？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐……
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary...

t_ch: 又有我的快递？
t_en: Another delivery for me?

t_ch: 没有，我现在只能拨通这个电话亭……
t_en: No, I can only get through to this phone booth right now...

t_ch: 挂断电话。
t_en: Hang up the call.

t_ch: 短暂的沉默后，旁边的答录机传来了玛丽小姐的声音。
t_en: After a brief silence, Miss Mary's voice comes from the answering machine beside.

t_ch: 哇——好狠心的人，又想把玛丽小姐遗弃不管了吗——
t_en: Waah— How heartless! Are you trying to abandon me again?

t_ch: 请帮帮玛丽小姐，我现在迷路了，这里好奇怪啊！
t_en: Please help me. I'm lost, and this place is so weird!

t_ch: 描述一下你现在周围的环境？
t_en: Can you describe your surroundings?

t_ch: 四周空空的，只有玛丽小姐脚底下有路，我感觉自己好像被压扁了！
t_en: It's empty all around, and there is only a path under my feet. I feel like I've been flattened into a pancake!

t_ch: 怎么会迷路的？
t_en: How did you get lost?

t_ch: 玛丽小姐刚刚在送信，但被路过的电车撞到，回过神就在这里了。
t_en: Miss Mary was just delivering mail when she got hit by a passing tram. When she came to, she found herself here.

t_ch: 总之先往前走吧。
t_en: Let's just keep moving for now.

t_ch: 好的，这里有点像二维市的二次元JUMP，玛丽小姐现在很慌，求求你千万不要挂电话——
t_en: Okay, this is kinda like an Interplanar Jump in Duomension City. Miss Mary is really freaking out right now, please please don't hang up—

t_ch: 呃…前面有个三岔路口，还有一块路牌！
t_en: Uh... There's a three-way intersection up ahead, and a road sign!

t_ch: 向左边的箭头旁画着一团蓝色的刺球，右边的画着一团粉色的圆球，中间的画着红色的帽子？
t_en: A spiky blue ball is drawn next to the arrow on the left. A round pink ball is drawn next to the arrow on the right. A red hat is drawn next to the arrow in the middle?

t_ch: 往左走！
t_en: Go left!

t_ch: 咦，我来到了一个新地方，看起来像是开发过的森林，路上还洒着很多圆环…戒指？
t_en: Huh? I've arrived at a new place. Looks like a forest that's been worked over, and there are lots of rings scattered on the path... Wait, rings?

t_ch: 而且…怎么到处都是过山车一样三百六十度的环形路面啊？
t_en: And why are there <unbreak>360</unbreak>-degree loop tracks like roller coasters everywhere?

t_ch: 只要你的速度够快就能走。
t_en: You can make it as long as you're fast enough.

t_ch: 你是对的，我看到蓝色的刺球正在那种路面上疾驰了。
t_en: You're right, I can see a spiky blue ball zooming across the tracks.

t_ch: 捡点戒指备用吧。
t_en: Pick up some rings to keep on hand.

t_ch: 很难想象戒指会有什么用处。等一下，我看到一个蓝色的刺球正在飞速移动——
t_en: Hard to imagine what I can use the rings for. Wait a sec, I see a blue spiky ball moving at breakneck speed—

t_ch: 快跑，会惹上官司的！
t_en: Run! You're gonna get sued!

t_ch: 什么？别吓我啊！等一下，我看到一个蓝色的刺球正在飞速移动——
t_en: What? Don't scare me like that! Wait a sec, I see a blue spiky ball moving at breakneck speed—

t_ch: 咦，怎么越来越近了，不要超速行驶——
t_en: Huh? Why is it getting closer and closer. Don't exceed the speed limit—

t_ch: 「砰咚！」
t_en: "BONK!"

t_ch: 往右走！
t_en: Go right!

t_ch: 这里是…好漂亮的地方…蓝天绿树，还有很多流星掉下来。玛丽小姐正在许愿回到二相乐园……
t_en: This place is... It's so pretty... The skies are so blue, the trees are so green, and there are so many shooting stars. Miss Mary is making a wish to return to Planarcadia...

t_ch: 我看到粉色的团子了，好可爱。它好像也是幻造种，正在打盹呢。我该怎么办？
t_en: I see a pink blob, it's adorable! I think it's an imagenae, and it's taking a nap. What should I do?

t_ch: 去跟它打个招呼？
t_en: Wanna go say hi?

t_ch: 我试试看…你好，可以打扰一下吗？…嗯嗯，它好像醒了。
t_en: Let me try... Hello, excuse me? ...Hmm, I think it's waking up.

t_ch: 去捡点星星备用吧。
t_en: Go pick up some stars to keep on hand.

t_ch: 能够被捡起来的星星，真是神秘。啊，粉团子好像醒过来了。
t_en: Stars that can be picked up, how mysterious. Ah, I think the pink blob is waking up.

t_ch: 快跑，会惹上官司的！
t_en: Run! You're gonna get sued!

t_ch: 什么？不要吓我啊！啊，粉色的团子好像醒了……
t_en: What? Don't scare me like that! Ah, I think the pink blob is waking up...

t_ch: 原来它的嘴巴能张这么大啊~等，等一下！玛丽小姐不是吃的！要被吸走了，哇——
t_en: Whoa, I had no idea its mouth could open that wide~ W—Wait a second! Miss Mary isn't food! I'm getting sucked in, waaah—

t_ch: 往中间走！
t_en: Go straight!

t_ch: 咦，我来到了一个新地方…这里有浮在天上的砖块，很多绿色的水管。
t_en: Huh? I've arrived at a new place. There are floating blocks in the sky and lots of green pipes everywhere.

t_ch: 还有很多乌龟和栗子一样的幻造种，我该怎么办？
t_en: There are also lots of imagenae that look like turtles and chestnuts... What should I do?

t_ch: 试着向它们问问路？
t_en: Maybe you can ask them for directions?

t_ch: 我试试看…你们好？吃饭了吗？
t_en: Let me try... Hey there! Have you eaten?

t_ch: …它们发出了一些咕噜咕噜的声音，玛丽小姐听不懂啦。
t_en: ...They made some gurgling noises. Miss Mary doesn't understand.

t_ch: 可以跳起来踩它们。
t_en: You can jump up and stomp on them.

t_ch: 玛丽小姐跳不了那么高啊！你为什么要这么说，难道说你知道这是哪里吗？
t_en: Miss Mary can't jump that high! Why would you say that anyway... Don't tell me you know where I am?

t_ch: 快跑，会惹上官司的！
t_en: Run! You're gonna get sued!

t_ch: 呀，它们朝我冲过来了！玛丽小姐要钻到前面的水管里去避难了！
t_en: Yikes, they're charging at me! Miss Mary's gonna dive into that pipe up ahead to take cover!

t_ch: 电话突兀地挂断了——以防万一，在电话亭里多呆一会吧，希望玛丽小姐一切安好。
t_en: The phone cuts off abruptly. Maybe you should stay in the phone booth a bit longer, just in case. Hopefully, Miss Mary is alright.

t_ch: 不久，电话再次响了起来。
t_en: Soon after, the phone rings again.

t_ch: 玛、玛丽小姐，现在、在泊地站。
t_en: M—Miss Mary reporting in, I'm at P—Perdido Station right now.

t_ch: 欢迎回到现实世界。
t_en: Welcome back to the real world.

t_ch: 你没事吧！
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 还，还好。玛丽小姐惊魂未定，要回去休息一下。不过在此之前……
t_en: I—I'm fine. I'm still quite shaken and needs some rest. But before that...

t_ch: 电话被挂断了，但很快又响了起来，你没来得及接起，答录机就已经响起了玛丽小姐的声音。
t_en: The call was cut off, but it rang again shortly after. Before you could pick up, the answering machine had already started playing Miss Mary's voice.

t_ch: 我是玛丽小姐，我现在 在 你 的 背  后~
t_en: This is Miss Mary, and I'm~ right~ behind~ you~

t_ch: 玛丽速递，马上就到你身后～
t_en: Mary Express, right behind you already~

t_ch: 从玛丽小姐那里得到了赞许，你感到一种充实感从心底升起，熠熠生辉。
t_en: Getting Miss Mary's approval fills you with a deep sense of fulfillment, making your heart glow with pride.

t_ch: 电话亭静静伫立着，尽力不发出任何悲鸣。它在为你逝去的快递，献上属于灵物的悼念。
t_en: The phone booth stands in solemn silence, doing its best not to let out a single mournful cry. It's offering a Faekind's grief for the delivery you'll never receive.

t_ch: 就让这段宁静——再持续些许时间吧。
t_en: Let this peace last... just a little while longer.

t_ch: 小小玛丽给你带来的惊悚体验仍历历在目…不，应该说是历历在耳。
t_en: The horror Mini Mary put you through is still fresh in your mind... No, make that fresh in your ears.

t_ch: 应该不会有好事之人给它打造真实的躯体吧…谁知道呢？
t_en: Surely no busybody would go and forge it a true body... who knows, though?

t_ch: 在这里，你曾聆听一场盛大的冒险，并亲自迎接故事的勇士主角回乡。
t_en: Here, you once listened to a grand adventure and personally welcomed the story's heroic protagonist home.

t_ch: 至于那段旅途的细节，就不要深究了。
t_en: As for the details of that journey, let's not dig too deep into it.

t_ch: 今天我们站在这里，来缅怀一位拿电话亭开玩笑的乐子人。
t_en: Today we gather here to commemorate a Fun Lovin' soul who made a joke out of the phone booth.

t_ch: 乐子神在上，愿他的欢愉之魂能够消停一会，至少下次别再惹玛丽小姐了。
t_en: By The Laughter above, may his soul of Elation be soothed awhile, that he might cease his torments of Miss Mary, at the very least.

t_ch: 喂？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 喂喂，你好，我是玛丽小姐，你为什么把我丢掉呢？我一定会去找你的。
t_en: Hey, hey! Hello there! This is Miss Mary speaking. Why'd you toss me out like that? I'm definitely gonna come find you!

t_ch: 我不记得我有把你丢掉啊？
t_en: I don't remember throwing you away though?

t_ch: 好狠心的人，不仅将玛丽遗弃在河里，还假装不记得这件事。
t_en: How cruel! Not only did they abandon Mary in the river, they're even pretending they don't remember doing it.

t_ch: 你是玛丽，请问玛丽是谁？
t_en: You are Mary, but who is Mary?

t_ch: 好狠心的人，连自己最喜欢的洋娃娃名字都不记得了。
t_en: How cruel! You don't even remember the name of your favorite doll.

t_ch: 你找错人了，这边挂了哈。
t_en: Wrong number. Hanging up now, bye!

t_ch: 别想走，电话亭的门已经自动焊死了，在我来之前，你哪都别想去。
t_en: Don't even think about leaving. The phone booth door's been automatically welded shut. You're not going anywhere until I get there.

t_ch: 玛丽小姐来帮你回忆一下。
t_en: Let me help you remember.

t_ch: 小时候，我是你最喜欢的洋娃娃。但是你长大了，你狠心将玛丽小姐放在衣橱积灰。
t_en: When you were little, I was your favorite doll. But you grew up, and heartlessly left me in the closet.

t_ch: 然后你搬了家，你没有带走我，反手将玛丽小姐扔到了垃圾桶里，就像是扔掉了一块烂布。
t_en: Then you moved away, leaving me behind, and even tossing me into the trashcan like some worthless rag.

t_ch: 现在，我现在就在垃圾桶里，我一定会去找你的。
t_en: Right now, I'm in the trashcan, and I'm definitely coming to find you.

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话亭已经被上锁了…难道我是真的撞上了二相乐园的都市怪谈了？
t_en: The phone booth is locked... Could I have actually stumbled upon one of Planarcadia's Urban Myths?

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 接电话。
t_en: Answer the call.

t_ch: 不接电话。
t_en: Ignore the call.

t_ch: 电话铃声不响了，但是答录机自动响起了玛丽小姐的声音…等等，电话亭里有这么复古的东西吗？
t_en: The phone stops ringing, but an answering machine kicks in with Miss Mary's voice... Wait, do phone booths even have something that retro anymore?

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在贝洛伯格下层区。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm currently in the Underworld of Belobog.

t_ch: 合着你要从那么老远赶过来啊？
t_en: Wait, so you're traveling all the way here from way over there?

t_ch: 所以你是在贝洛伯格的垃圾桶？
t_en: So you're in a trashcan in Belobog?

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 接电话。
t_en: Answer the call.

t_ch: 不接电话。
t_en: Ignore the call.

t_ch: 电话铃声不响了，但是答录机自动响起了玛丽小姐的声音。
t_en: The phone stops ringing, but an answering machine kicks in with Miss Mary's voice.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在贝洛伯格下层区等电车。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm waiting for the tram in Belobog's Underworld right now.

t_ch: 那真是有点太远了，在你赶过来之前，我可能要被困死在电话亭里。
t_en: That's way too far. I might end up trapped to death in this phone booth before you even make it here.

t_ch: 你不要着急嘛，明明是玛丽小姐才是那个赶电车的人。
t_en: Hey, take it easy! I am the one who needs to catch the train, not you.

t_ch: 你有没有听说过一句话叫「慢慢走，欣赏啊」，现在玛丽小姐就在一边缓慢前进，一边欣赏沿途风景。人生的路，还很长呢。
t_en: Ever heard the saying "take it slow and smell the roses"? That's exactly what I am doing right now: moving at my own pace and taking in the sights along the way. After all, life's journey is a long one.

t_ch: 你能不能稍微快进一下？
t_en: Could you hurry up a little?

t_ch: 真正的恐怖是无尽等待…
t_en: True terror is the endless wait...

t_ch: 你放我出来吧，我找你。
t_en: Let me out. I'll find you.

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在泊地站。别急，我这就来找你。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm at Perdido Station right now. Don't worry. I'm on my way to find you.

t_ch: 快来，求你了。
t_en: Come on, please!

t_ch: 我天，好快啊！
t_en: Oh my gosh, that was fast!

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。但电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The call's been cut off. But the phone started ringing again.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，我 现 在 在 你 身 后…
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm... right... behind... you...

t_ch: 玛丽速递，马上就到你身后～
t_en: Mary Express, right behind you already~

t_ch: 从玛丽小姐那里得到的，是来自贝洛伯格的一封信，署名垃圾桶之王。
t_en: What you got from Miss Mary was a letter from Belobog, signed by none other than the King of Trashcans.

t_ch: 「想你的风还是吹到了二相乐园，没事的时候常回冬城看看。」
t_en: "The wind carrying my love for you has finally reached Planarcadia. Come back to Everwinter City to visit when you have time."

t_ch: 我那么大个玛丽小姐人呢？
t_en: Wait, where did Miss Mary go!?

t_ch: 只能期待下次相遇了。
t_en: Guess we'll just have to catch up next time!

t_ch: 谢谢你，老朋友，我也想你。
t_en: Thanks, old friend. I've missed you, too.

t_ch: 千里传书信，这就是友情的重量。
t_en: Letters traveling thousands of miles, that's what friendship weighs.

t_ch: 合着你要从那么老远赶过来啊？
t_en: Wait, so you're traveling all the way here from way over there?

t_ch: 所以你是在贝洛伯格的垃圾桶？
t_en: So you're in a trashcan in Belobog?

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 接电话。
t_en: Answer the call.

t_ch: 不接电话。
t_en: Ignore the call.

t_ch: 电话铃声不响了，但是答录机自动响起了玛丽小姐的声音。
t_en: The phone stops ringing, but an answering machine kicks in with Miss Mary's voice.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在贝洛伯格下层区等电车。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm waiting for the tram in Belobog's Underworld right now.

t_ch: 那真是有点太远了，在你赶过来之前，我可能要被困死在电话亭里。
t_en: That's way too far. I might end up trapped to death in this phone booth before you even make it here.

t_ch: 你不要着急嘛，明明是玛丽小姐才是那个赶电车的人。
t_en: Hey, take it easy! I am the one who needs to catch the train, not you.

t_ch: 你有没有听说过一句话叫「慢慢走，欣赏啊」，现在玛丽小姐就在一边缓慢前进，一边欣赏沿途风景。人生的路，还很长呢。
t_en: Ever heard the saying "take it slow and smell the roses"? That's exactly what I am doing right now: moving at my own pace and taking in the sights along the way. After all, life's journey is a long one.

t_ch: 你能不能稍微快进一下？
t_en: Could you hurry up a little?

t_ch: 真正的恐怖是无尽等待…
t_en: True terror is the endless wait...

t_ch: 你放我出来吧，我找你。
t_en: Let me out. I'll find you.

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在泊地站。别急，我这就来找你。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm at Perdido Station right now. Don't worry. I'm on my way to find you.

t_ch: 快来，求你了。
t_en: Come on, please!

t_ch: 我天，好快啊！
t_en: Oh my gosh, that was fast!

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。但电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The call's been cut off. But the phone started ringing again.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，我 现 在 在 你 身 后…
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm... right... behind... you...

t_ch: 玛丽速递，马上就到你身后～
t_en: Mary Express, right behind you already~

t_ch: 从玛丽小姐那里得到的，是来自贝洛伯格的一封信，署名垃圾桶之王。
t_en: What you got from Miss Mary was a letter from Belobog, signed by none other than the King of Trashcans.

t_ch: 「想你的风还是吹到了二相乐园，没事的时候常回冬城看看。」
t_en: "The wind carrying my love for you has finally reached Planarcadia. Come back to Everwinter City to visit when you have time."

t_ch: 我那么大个玛丽小姐人呢？
t_en: Wait, where did Miss Mary go!?

t_ch: 只能期待下次相遇了。
t_en: Guess we'll just have to catch up next time!

t_ch: 谢谢你，老朋友，我也想你。
t_en: Thanks, old friend. I've missed you, too.

t_ch: 千里传书信，这就是友情的重量。
t_en: Letters traveling thousands of miles, that's what friendship weighs.

t_ch: 喂？
t_en: Hello?

t_ch: 喂喂，你好，我是玛丽小姐，你为什么把我丢掉呢？我一定会去找你的。
t_en: Hey, hey! Hello there! This is Miss Mary speaking. Why'd you toss me out like that? I'm definitely gonna come find you!

t_ch: 我不记得我有把你丢掉啊？
t_en: I don't remember throwing you away though?

t_ch: 好狠心的人，不仅将玛丽遗弃在河里，还假装不记得这件事。
t_en: How cruel! Not only did they abandon Mary in the river, they're even pretending they don't remember doing it.

t_ch: 你是玛丽，请问玛丽是谁？
t_en: You are Mary, but who is Mary?

t_ch: 好狠心的人，连自己最喜欢的洋娃娃名字都不记得了。
t_en: How cruel! You don't even remember the name of your favorite doll.

t_ch: 你找错人了，这边挂了哈。
t_en: Wrong number. Hanging up now, bye!

t_ch: 别想走，电话亭的门已经自动焊死了，在我来之前，你哪都别想去。
t_en: Don't even think about leaving. The phone booth door's been automatically welded shut. You're not going anywhere until I get there.

t_ch: 玛丽小姐来帮你回忆一下。
t_en: Let me help you remember.

t_ch: 小时候，我是你最喜欢的洋娃娃。但是你长大了，你狠心将玛丽小姐放在衣橱积灰。
t_en: When you were little, I was your favorite doll. But you grew up, and heartlessly left me in the closet.

t_ch: 然后你搬了家，你没有带走我，反手将玛丽小姐扔到了垃圾桶里，就像是扔掉了一块烂布。
t_en: Then you moved away, leaving me behind, and even tossing me into the trashcan like some worthless rag.

t_ch: 现在，我现在就在垃圾桶里，我一定会去找你的。
t_en: Right now, I'm in the trashcan, and I'm definitely coming to find you.

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话亭已经被上锁了…难道我是真的撞上了二相乐园的都市怪谈了？
t_en: The phone booth is locked... Could I have actually stumbled upon one of Planarcadia's Urban Myths?

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 接电话。
t_en: Answer the call.

t_ch: 不接电话。
t_en: Ignore the call.

t_ch: 电话铃声不响了，但是答录机自动响起了玛丽小姐的声音…等等，电话亭里有这么复古的东西吗？
t_en: The phone stops ringing, but an answering machine kicks in with Miss Mary's voice... Wait, do phone booths even have something that retro anymore?

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在贝洛伯格下层区。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm currently in the Underworld of Belobog.

t_ch: 合着你要从那么老远赶过来啊？
t_en: Wait, so you're traveling all the way here from way over there?

t_ch: 所以你是在贝洛伯格的垃圾桶？
t_en: So you're in a trashcan in Belobog?

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。
t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 接电话。
t_en: Answer the call.

t_ch: 不接电话。
t_en: Ignore the call.

t_ch: 电话铃声不响了，但是答录机自动响起了玛丽小姐的声音。
t_en: The phone stops ringing, but an answering machine kicks in with Miss Mary's voice.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在贝洛伯格下层区等电车。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm waiting for the tram in Belobog's Underworld right now.

t_ch: 那真是有点太远了，在你赶过来之前，我可能要被困死在电话亭里。
t_en: That's way too far. I might end up trapped to death in this phone booth before you even make it here.

t_ch: 你不要着急嘛，明明是玛丽小姐才是那个赶电车的人。
t_en: Hey, take it easy! I am the one who needs to catch the train, not you.

t_ch: 你有没有听说过一句话叫「慢慢走，欣赏啊」，现在玛丽小姐就在一边缓慢前进，一边欣赏沿途风景。人生的路，还很长呢。

t_en: Could you hurry up a little?

t_en: True terror is the endless wait...

t_en: Let me out. I'll find you.

t_en: The call's been cut off.

t_ch: 电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The phone rings again.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，现在我就在泊地站。别急，我这就来找你。
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm at Perdido Station right now. Don't worry. I'm on my way to find you.

t_ch: 快来，求你了。
t_en: Come on, please!

t_ch: 我天，好快啊！
t_en: Oh my gosh, that was fast!

t_ch: 电话被挂断了。但电话铃再次响了起来。
t_en: The call's been cut off. But the phone started ringing again.

t_ch: 你好，我是玛丽小姐，我 现 在 在 你 身 后…
t_en: Hi, this is Miss Mary, and I'm... right... behind... you...

t_ch: 玛丽速递，马上就到你身后～
t_en: Mary Express, right behind you already~

t_ch: 从玛丽小姐那里得到的，是来自贝洛伯格的一封信，署名垃圾桶之王。
t_en: What you got from Miss Mary was a letter from Belobog, signed by none other than the King of Trashcans.

t_ch: 「想你的风还是吹到了二相乐园，没事的时候常回冬城看看。」
t_en: "The wind carrying my love for you has finally reached Planarcadia. Come back to Everwinter City to visit when you have time."

t_ch: 我那么大个玛丽小姐人呢？
t_en: Wait, where did Miss Mary go!?

t_ch: 只能期待下次相遇了。
t_en: Guess we'll just have to catch up next time!

t_ch: 谢谢你，老朋友，我也想你。
t_en: Thanks, old friend. I've missed you, too.

t_ch: 千里传书信，这就是友情的重量。
t_en: Letters traveling thousands of miles, that's what friendship weighs.

t_ch: 这是一扇门。
t_en: This is a door.

t_ch: 「这是一扇门」有些太过于理所当然了，问题是在于——这里只有*一扇门*，或者说，这里只剩下了*一扇门*，门前门后都完全可见，所以它是来干什么的……
t_en: "This is a door" is stating the obvious. Here's the problem: there's only *one door* here. Or rather, only *one door* remains, with both sides of it completely visible. So what exactly is it for...

t_ch: 也许不用思考得那么复杂——门在打开的时候是「通道」，门在关闭的时候是「屏障」。现在只需要去打开它，然后走进门里，你就是知道它究竟是何处的通道、又是何处的屏障了。
t_en: Perhaps there's no need to overthink it. A door is a "passage" when it's open; it's a "barrier" when it's shut. Right now, all you have to do is open it and walk through. Then you'll know where exactly it leads, and where it keeps you from.

t_ch: 打开这扇门。
t_en: Open this door.

t_ch: 不详的漩涡…或许你曾在蓝色的猫型机器人那里看到过类似的门。
t_en: An ominous vortex... Perhaps you've seen a blue, cat-shaped robot in possession of a similar door.

t_ch: …唯一的差别是，它知道自己要去哪，你不知道。
t_en: ...The only difference is, it knows where it's going, but you don't.

t_ch: 但是这又何妨呢，难不成你还会被绑架不成？去吧！
t_en: But what's the worst that could happen? It's not like you're gonna get kidnapped, are you? Go for it!

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 好奇心曾经害死了不少的生物，而你活下来了！这说明了你的谨慎是有道理的…但是，门就在这里，欢迎你在任何时候打开它。
t_en: Curiosity has claimed the lives of many creatures before you, but you survived! This proves your caution was well-founded... But still, the door is right here, and you're welcome to open it anytime.

t_ch: 喂，醒醒——
t_en: Hey, wake up—

t_ch: 你被绑架了。
t_en: You've been kidnapped.

t_ch: 仔细一看，这不是愚者们热烈讨论的大红人{NICKNAME}吗？你为什么会穿门而过呢？
t_en: Let me take a closer look. Hey, isn't this {NICKNAME}, the talk of the town among the Fools? Why did you walk right through that door?

t_ch: 因为门就在那里。
t_en: Because the door is right there.

t_ch: 多么简单而直接的理由，就像一名抢劫犯说「因为钱就在那里」。
t_en: What a simple and straightforward reason. It's like a robber saying "because the money was right there."

t_ch: 是为了来这见你。
t_en: I came here to see you.

t_ch: 真的吗？我好开心，竟然是这种理由！
t_en: Really? I'm so happy, I can't believe that's the reason!

t_ch: 但是…你根本就不认识我。在你眼中，我只是「不认识的声音」。
t_en: But... you don't even know who I am. To you, I'm just an "unfamiliar voice."

t_ch: 不小心就跑进来了。
t_en: I accidentally walked right in.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，那还真是不小心啊…你以为我会相信吗？
t_en: HAHA, an "accident" you say... You really think I'd buy that?

t_ch: 我是「制门匠」，这里是酒馆的藏品室。而你刚才穿过的门，也是这间藏品室的藏品之一，由我亲手制作的「阿门」。
t_en: I am the "Doormaker," and this is the collection room of the tavern. The door you just passed through is also one of the pieces in this collection. "A-Door," crafted by my own hands.

t_ch: 阿、阿门？
t_en: A-what? A-Door?

t_ch: 阿哈的任意门，简称「阿门」。
t_en: Aha's Anywhere Door. "A-Door" for short.

t_ch: 阿门建立在伪科学之上，你可以说它的原理是虫洞、量子隧道、时空弯曲——但我们更愿意称呼它为「阿哈的伟力」。
t_en: "A-Door" is built on pseudoscience. You could say it works through wormholes, quantum tunneling, or space-time warping, but we prefer to call it "The Power of Aha."

t_ch: 阿门的外型可以是个衣橱，也可以是个兔子洞…但幸好，这一批丢失的门都还没化过妆。
t_en: A-Door's appearance could be a wardrobe, or a rabbit hole... but thankfully, all these doors are still free of makeup.

t_ch: 制门匠？
t_en: "Doormaker"?

t_ch: 没错，这是我的代称。酒馆里不同的愚者们自由出入不同的位面，就是靠着阿门。
t_en: That's right, that's my alias. The different Fools in the tavern can traverse different planes freely thanks to A-Door.

t_ch: 而「制门匠」就是负责制作、管理和维修这些「门」的人。
t_en: And "Doormakers" are the ones responsible for creating, managing, and maintaining these "doors."

t_ch: 所以，你为什么要绑架我？
t_en: So, why did you kidnap me?

t_ch: 那只是我随口一说，你并没有被绑架，你只是舒适地坐在马桶上。
t_en: I was just kidding. You weren't kidnapped, you were just sitting comfortably on the toilet.

t_ch: 放心吧，酒馆的人告诉过我了，你不仅不是威胁，还是酒馆的好朋友。而我，只是想在你离开前，确认「门」的情况。
t_en: Don't worry, the folks at the tavern told me you're no threat. In fact, they said you're a good friend of the tavern. As for me, I just wanted to check on the status of the "door" before you leave.

t_ch: 你们简单地交换了有关「门」的情报。
t_en: You briefly exchanged information about the "door."

t_ch: 前不久，有好事的愚者把收藏室里低品质的「门」全都打开了，然后它们又在另一侧被好事者关上…总之，这些门就这样从酒馆里逃逸了。
t_en: Not long ago, some meddlesome Fool opened all the low-quality "doors" in the collection room, only for them to be shut again by another meddler on the other side... Just like that, these doors escaped from the tavern.

t_ch: 而你眼前的这扇门，是我造出来专门用来回收那些半成品的「门」。今天，它终于成功截获了一名过门的人。为了表达谢意，你被允许使用这扇阿门了。
t_en: And this door before you? I built it specifically to retrieve those half-baked "doors." Today, it finally succeeded in intercepting someone passing through. As a token of gratitude, you're now granted permission to use this A-Door door.

t_ch: 如果你在旅行过程中发现了「门」，也请你直接通过它，这样的话，我便可以锁定门两侧的坐标，完成门的定位。
t_en: If you encounter a "door" during your journey, please go through it directly. This way, I can pinpoint the coordinates on both sides of the door and complete its location mapping.

t_ch: 所以，你是打算回收这些门？
t_en: So, you're planning to retrieve these doors?

t_ch: 不了，就丢在外面吧，回收起来还蛮麻烦的，这世界出点乱子没什么大不了的。
t_en: Nah, just leave them outside. They're a hassle to retrieve anyway. A little chaos in the world is no big deal.

t_ch: …只要能在世界毁灭者到来之前把门全部都回收了就行，应该没问题的。
t_en: ...as long as all the doors are retrieved before the World Destroyer arrives, it should be fine.

t_ch: 需要我专门跑过来告诉你吗？
t_en: Do I need to come all the way here to tell you in person?

t_ch: 不用了，这些门还是有开门记录的，只要被再次打开过，就能追踪。
t_en: No need, these doors still keep access records. As long as they've been opened again, we can track them.

t_ch: 我这个人不打白工。
t_en: I don't work for free.

t_ch: 当然，你不会白白冒着未知的风险打开门。若我们的定位工作进展顺利，你会拿到我的奖励。
t_en: Of course, you won't be taking these risks for nothing. If our locating work goes smoothly, you'll get your remuneration.

t_ch: 等你找得差不多了，你再回来找我吧，我就在这扇门的背后。
t_en: Come back once you've found enough. I'll be right behind this door.

t_ch: 这是一扇阿门。
t_en: This is an A-Door.

t_ch: 敲门。
t_en: Knock.

t_ch: 怎么了吗，门外汉，找我有事？
t_en: What's the matter, outsider? Need something from me?

t_ch: 随便聊聊呗。
t_en: Let's chat for a bit.

t_ch: 可以，不过我们只能隔着门这样聊了。你知道的，这扇阿门不通到我这。
t_en: Sure, but we'll have to chat through the door like this. You know, this A-Door doesn't connect to where I am.

t_ch: 阿门是什么？
t_en: What's an A-Door?

t_ch: 阿哈的任意门，简称「阿门」。它建立在伪科学之上，你可以说它的原理是虫洞、量子隧道、时空弯曲——但我们更愿意称呼它为「阿哈的伟力」。
t_en: Aha's Anywhere Door, or "A-Door" for short. It's built on pseudoscience. You could say it works through wormholes, quantum tunneling, or space-time warping, but we prefer to call it "The Power of Aha."

t_ch: 制门匠是什么？
t_en: What's a Doormaker?

t_ch: 很简单，「制门匠」是负责制作、管理和维修阿门的人。
t_en: A Doormaker is simply someone who creates, manages, and maintains the A-Door.

t_ch: 你有什么作品？
t_en: What have you made?

t_ch: 鸽川水里那扇门很酷吧？嘿嘿。
t_en: You know that pretty cool door in Dovebrook's waters? That's me, hehe.

t_ch: 没什么好聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 一个小建议——要是你想聊天了，二维市市内的神人还蛮多的。
t_en: A little tip: If you're looking to chat, there are quite a few colorful characters in Duomension City.

t_ch: 我想领取奖励。
t_en: I want to claim my reward.

t_ch: 让我看看开门记录。
t_en: Let me check the door access logs.

t_ch: 多亏了你，我们定位到所有逃逸的阿门。这是我许诺过的奖励，请收下。
t_en: Thanks to you, we've located all wayward A-Doors. Here's the reward I promised, please take it.

t_ch: 现在你开过的阿门数量还不够，等你收集了足够多的阿门，再来找我吧。
t_en: You haven't opened enough A-Doors yet. Come back to me when you've found more of them.

t_ch: 我都发过你奖励了，你别再想讹我了。
t_en: I already gave you your reward, so don't try to scam me.

t_ch: 没事了，我走了。
t_en: Well, I'm off then.

t_ch: 推门。
t_en: Push the door.

t_ch: 阿门是一扇传送门，要传送哪里，阿哈可不管。
t_en: "A-Door" is a teleporter, and "A-ha" couldn't care less where it teleports you to.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 你有些纳闷为什么「制门匠」身旁的阿门会通向这里。
t_en: You're somewhat puzzled by why the A-Door next to the Doormaker leads here.

t_ch: 可能是为了更方便倾吐烦恼吧……
t_en: Maybe it makes venting more convenient...

t_ch: 这是一扇阿门。
t_en: This is an A-Door.

t_ch: 推门而入。
t_en: Push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 推不开…似乎有什么神秘的力量正在和你唱反调——
t_en: The A-Door won't budge... seems like some mysterious force is working against you...

t_ch: 「你没资格啊，你没资格——」
t_en: "You don't have the right, O you don't have the right..."

t_ch: 轻轻敲门。
t_en: Knock gently.

t_ch: 锁链被拖拽而过，机杼扭转喀哒，金属囚门相撞……
t_en: Chains dragged across the floor, gears twisting and contorting, metal prison doors slamming shut...

t_ch: 门后传来了一些让人背后发凉的声音。
t_en: You hear some spine-chilling noises from behind the door.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 这里应该是你很熟悉的监狱，罗浮仙舟的幽囚狱。而你不熟悉的是，门的另一端为什么会在这里。
t_en: This should be the familiar Shackling Prison of the Xianzhou Luofu to you. However, what's unfamiliar is why the A-Door would lead here.

t_ch: 这位一直在打量着你的官方工作人员保持着微笑，她满脸写着：怎么，吃完饭没事来监狱自首来了？
t_en: The officer keeps a polite smile while sizing you up, her face clearly saying: What's this? Turning yourself in for fun?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，我们又见面了，我是负责这片幽囚狱的司狱赤衣。我看得出你对自己为什么会出现在幽囚狱感到疑惑，我丝毫不感到意外。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we meet again. I am Chiyi, the Jailer in charge of this section of The Shackling Prison. I can see your confusion as to why you've ended up here, and I'm not surprised in the slightest.

t_ch: 幽囚狱一直都有提防着「靠着纯粹随机的方式定位到幽囚狱内部的双向传送门」，我们对这里的空间坐标做了一些调整，保证那些冒失的传送门总能到达这间专属囚室。
t_en: The Shackling Prison has always been wary of "two-way teleporters that randomly lock onto coordinates within the prison." Thus, we've made adjustments to the spatial coordinates here to ensure that rogue teleporters always deliver their subjects to this dedicated cell.

t_ch: 当然，这都是为了防范越狱的必要举措。
t_en: Naturally, these are all necessary precautions to prevent prison breaks.

t_ch: 你不会要抓我去坐牢吧？
t_en: You're not gonna throw me in jail now, are you?

t_ch: 如果是一般的位面旅行者，我会先抓后审。
t_en: The standard procedure for a typical dimensional traveler would be to detain and interrogate.

t_ch: 但我认识您，我能想象您大概是在某个世界随意地打开了一扇门，然后再恍然大悟发现自己已经在牢里了。我说的没错对吧？
t_en: However, knowing you, I'd imagine you casually opening an inconspicuous door in some other world, just to find yourself suddenly behind bars. Does that sound about right?

t_ch: 看来你们抓了不少传送者？
t_en: Looks like you've caught quite a few travelers, then?

t_ch: 我们确实抓住过一些冒失的位面旅行者，不过嘛，更多的则是策划越狱却自称是来这旅游的人。
t_en: Careless ones, yes, but more often than not, individuals who plan the escapes while claiming to be here "on vacation."

t_ch: 我自然是相信{NICKNAME}大人的，不过我的工作习惯促使我要再次提醒您「幽囚狱并非是一个来去自由的场所」，大人切要小心行事。
t_en: I am more than happy to put my trust in you, {NICKNAME}. But it is my professional obligation to remind you, once again, that The Shackling Prison is not a place where people walk about freely. Please be cautious in your operations here.

t_ch: 至于这扇传送门嘛，过些时日，我们会将其安全销毁。{NICKNAME}大人，现在您可以通过那扇门回去，不过既然*不小心*回到了罗浮，不如上街逛逛去。
t_en: As for this teleporter, we'll safely dispose of it in a few days. You may return through it now, {NICKNAME}, but since you've "accidentally" found yourself back on the Luofu, why not take a stroll around town?

t_ch: 没有别的事情，那么，赤衣就此告辞了。
t_en: If there's nothing else, I shall take my leave.

t_ch: 这是一扇阿门。
t_en: This is an A-Door.

t_ch: 推门回去。
t_en: Push open the door and go back.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 一扇阿门旁若无人地立在街边。
t_en: A lone A-Door stands nonchalantly in the alley.

t_ch: 外头行人络绎，而它遗世独立。
t_en: Aloof and detached from the world's busy rally.

t_ch: 好可疑啊……
t_en: That's kinda sus...

t_ch: 推门而入。
t_en: Push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 轻轻敲门。
t_en: Knock gently.

t_ch: 就像以静默应对这个世界，它也以静默应对你。
t_en: Just as it meets the world with silence, it greets you with silence.

t_ch: 感觉更可疑了……
t_en: That's even more sus...

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 成了！终于成了——
t_en: Yes! Finally, it worked...!

t_ch: 发出一声很酷，但也充满疑惑的*哼*！
t_en: Let out a cool but puzzled "hmph"!

t_ch: 别紧张，朋友。我不是什么好人，但也不是什么坏人，我是…「钟表小子」。
t_en: Easy there, friend. I'm no saint, but I'm not a villain either. I am... "Clockie."

t_ch: 而你现在是我的搭档，我的挚友，我最忠诚的好伙伴…「哈努兄弟」。
t_en: And now you're my partner, my dear friend, most loyal companion... "Brother Hanu."

t_ch: 这还是二相乐园吗？
t_en: Is this still Planarcadia?

t_ch: 发出一声诧异的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a surprised "hmph."

t_ch: 都叫兄弟了，还说啥呢。
t_en: He's already calling you "Brother." C'mon.

t_ch: 发出一声真性情的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a heartfelt "hmph."

t_ch: 发出一声很酷的*哼*！
t_en: Let out a really cool sounding "hmph"!

t_ch: 啊哈，你已经完全代入角色了！
t_en: Aha! You're completely in character now!

t_ch: 我知道你现在可能想要交流，但现在想要交流确实不太可能…不过别担心，虽然你说的话我听不懂，但我说的话你听得懂。
t_en: I know you probably have a lot to say, but that's not really possible at the moment... Don't worry though, while I can't understand what you're saying, at least you understand what I'm saying.

t_ch: 你眼前这扇门，原本只是一扇平平无奇的传送门。很无趣，很无聊。而我在它的数据库里导入了「克劳克影业」旗下的全部IP形象，好精彩，好好玩！
t_en: This door in front of you was just an ordinary, run-of-the-mill teleporter. So dull, so boring. But then I imported all the IP characters from Clock Studios into its database! So exciting, much fun!

t_ch: 现在，它能在传送的同时把人变成特定IP形象，摇身一变，成为「阿•cosplay•门」。
t_en: Now, on top of teleporting someone, it can also transform them into specific IP characters. It has become... "A-Cosplay-Door."

t_ch: 想象一下，假如把这扇门悄悄安装到那些大型漫展的入口…所有漫展都将变成克劳克ONLY！
t_en: Just imagine... If we secretly installed this door at the entrance of those big comic conventions... all conventions would become Clock-Studios-only!

t_ch: 我们克劳克粉丝的腰杆在二相乐园，从来没有挺得这么直过。
t_en: We Clock Studios fans have never held our heads so high before in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 真不枉我冒着被制门匠丢到宇宙尽头的风险，帮那些在收藏室吃灰的阿门们来一场说走就走的旅行……
t_en: It was worth the risk of being thrown to the universe's end by the Doormaker, freeing these dusty A-Doors in the collection room and taking them on an unplanned excursion...

t_ch: 原来是你小子干的…
t_en: So it was you...

t_ch: 等着宇宙尽头见吧！
t_en: Say hello to the edge of the universe for me!

t_ch: 发出一声正义凛然的*哼*！
t_en: Let out a righteous "hmph"!

t_ch: 哦，不要这么紧张，朋友。虽然变化的效果还不太稳定，但绝对不会有生命危险，顶多……
t_en: Oh, don't be so nervous, friend. While the transformation effects aren't quite stable yet, it's one hundred percent safe! At worst, it'll—

t_ch: 发出一声很酷的*哼*！
t_en: Let out a really cool sounding "hmph"!

t_ch: …效果确实不太稳定。
t_en: ...The effects are indeed quite volatile.

t_ch: 很可惜，「阿•cosplay•门」成功了，但没有完全成功。
t_en: A shame. The A-Cosplay-Door succeeded, but it was a partial success.

t_ch: 发出一声很酷的*哼*！
t_en: Let out a really cool sounding "hmph"!

t_ch: 你知道他现在可能想要交流，但现在想要交流确实不太可能。
t_en: You know he probably has a lot to say, but that's not really possible at the moment.

t_ch: 发出一声充满遗憾的*哼*。
t_en: Lets out a regretful "hmph".

t_ch: 这是一扇阿门。
t_en: This is an A-Door.

t_ch: 推门回去。
t_en: Push open the door and go back.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 「学校的走廊上矗立着一扇陌生的门。没人知道它通往哪里，可打开它的人，没有一个回来…」还没见到这扇阿门，你就已经听说了有关它的怪谈。
t_en: "A mysterious door stands in the school hallway. No one knows where it leads, and those who opened it... never came back." The reputation of this ominous A-Door precedes it.
